meaning: Now pay attention! (introducing an important statement)
in order to bring us close
Allah (as the object being referred to)
between them (referring to those who took protectors besides Allah)
surely he would have chosen for himself
His absolute perfection (as an exclamation of praise)
The One, The Unique (name of Allah, subject of this part of the ayah)
The Supreme Subduer (one of Allah's names)
he wraps/rolls up (referring to how Allah overlaps day and night)
and He thoroughly controlled (the sun and moon)
The Mighty One (one of Allah's names)
The Most Forgiving One (one of Allah's names)
he created you all (referring to all humans)
of pairs/kinds (in this ayah: eight kinds of cattle)
a creation (being acted upon in this ayah)
that (far, with extra emphasis, speaking to multiple people)
your (all of you) Lord and Nurturer
for His servants (referring to Allah's servants)
he is pleased with it (referring to being grateful)
and he will inform you all
he granted him generously (referring to the human who was given a blessing from Allah)
a blessing (as the object being bestowed)
what (he used to call upon/invoke before)
rivals/equals (being set up as rivals to Allah)
so that he makes (someone) wander off the right path
Is the one who... (introducing a rhetorical question)
one who is devoted in worship
in a state of bowing down in worship
those who (referring to those who know in this ayah)
they know (referring to those who have knowledge)
the minds (referring to those who deeply understand)
be mindful/protect yourselves! (command to a group)
sincerely/with pure intention (describing how I worship)
for them (referring to those who fear their Lord)
the rivers (that flow under the lofty dwellings in paradise)
water (as the object being sent down)
and then He made it [the water] follow paths (into springs in the earth)
then (connecting two events with a time gap)
its colors (referring to the plant's colors)
he makes it (referring to the plant/crop mentioned earlier)
into broken pieces (what the plants are turned into)
that (far) (pointing to the previous examples in the ayah)
their hearts (the hearts of those who are hard-hearted, mentioned in the previous part of the ayah)
they fear (referring to those who fear Allah in this ayah)
that (pointing to Allah's Book mentioned before)
then it (the punishment) came to them
and so He made them taste
if only (they knew what Allah had planned)
they know (referring to the disbelievers who don't understand the parable)
indeed you (the one who will die)
those who are certainly dead/destined to die (plural, emphasized)
then (connects this statement to what came before)
those (who brought and believed in the truth)
their Lord/Master (referring to the good-doers mentioned after)
that (pointing to what was mentioned before)
reward/compensation (as the subject of the sentence)
and he gives/will give them what they deserve
with [One who is] sufficient (referring to Allah)
you asked them (referring to the disbelievers being asked about who created the heavens and earth)
the heavens (plural, in a possessive or connected state)
you (plural) call/invite/pray to (referring to the idols/deities being called upon besides Allah)
if (introduces condition about if Allah intends harm)
are they? (referring to the false gods)
or (connecting two possibilities: if Allah intends harm OR mercy)
female holders/withholders (referring to false deities that cannot hold back Allah's mercy)
a punishment/torment (that will come to him)
he found the right way (for himself)
and then He holds back (the souls that Allah has decreed death for)
and he sends (the other souls back)
they reflect (people who think about Allah's signs)
or (introducing a question about the second of two choices)
a thing (that they do not possess)
and when (connects to previous statement about disbelievers)
they (the hearts) were filled with intense disgust
and when (connects to previous statement about disbelievers)
they wronged (those who would try to ransom themselves)
all together (describing how everything in the earth would be used as ransom)
they would surely seek to ransom themselves
they take into account (what they weren't expecting from Allah)
they gained/earned (referring to the evil consequences that appeared to them)
he called out to us (when harm touched him)
I was given it (referring to the blessing/favor mentioned before)
he said it (referring to previous statement)
before them (referring to those who came earlier)
they earn/are earning (referring to those who came before them)
and so it afflicted/struck them (referring to those mentioned before)
among those who make others unable
O My servants! (addressing those who belong to Allah)
all together (describing how the sins are forgiven)
The Most Merciful (one of Allah's names)
and submit yourselves! (command to a group)
I was extremely negligent (in following Allah's commands)
if (introduces a wish that didn't happen)
that (emphasizing what follows is definitely true)
it will most certainly become void/worthless
and surely you will definitely be/exist
by His right hand (referring to Allah)
the earth (which will shine with its Lord's light)
between them (referring to the judgment between people on the Day of Judgment)
with the truth (in judging between them)
Hell (as the destination being driven to)
and he said (spoken by the guardians of Hell)
to/for them (the keepers of Hell speak to the disbelievers)
he comes to you all (addressing the disbelievers)
from you all (messengers who came to)
your (all) Lord (the Lord of the disbelievers being addressed)
your day (referring to the Day of Judgment)
they said (the disbelievers responding to the keepers of Hell)
the disbelievers (who are being driven to Hell)
their Lord (the one who cares for them)
the Paradise (referring to the eternal garden in the hereafter)
to them (referring to the guards of Paradise)
you all became pure/good (said by the guardians of Paradise to its people)
ones who continue/last forever (describing those who feared their Lord)
the angels (as the object being seen)
between them (referring to the angels mentioned earlier)
